{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks for the diagnosis of the given ECG. The documents provide information about various ECG findings and their clinical significance. Option A, ventricular bigeminy, refers to a regular alternation between a normal and an extra heartbeat originating from the ventricles. Option B, electrical alternans, involves varying changes in the amplitude of the QRS complex. Option C, P pulmonale, is a tall P wave in lead II or V1, indicative of right atrial enlargement. Option D, left ventricular failure, is characterized by a variety of ECG findings such as left axis deviation, RS ratio of 1 or less in lead V5 or V6, and tall P waves in lead II or V1. Given the information provided, the ECG findings align most closely with left ventricular failure.", "answer_choice": "D"}
